comment_text.sn23.4:1.2: The next seven discourses are the same as SN 22.106–12, but addressed to Rādha.

root_text.sn23.4:1.2: Āyasmā rādho yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ rādhaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn23.4:2.1: “Pariññeyye ca, rādha, dhamme desessāmi pariññañca pariññātāviṁ puggalañca.
root_text.sn23.4:2.2: Taṁ suṇāhi, sādhukaṁ manasi karohi; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.sn23.4:2.3: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho āyasmā rādho bhagavato paccassosi.
root_text.sn23.4:2.6: Rūpaṁ kho, rādha, pariññeyyo dhammo, vedanā pariññeyyo dhammo, saññā pariññeyyo dhammo, saṅkhārā pariññeyyo dhammo, viññāṇaṁ pariññeyyo dhammo.
root_text.sn23.4:2.9: Yo kho, rādha, rāgakkhayo dosakkhayo mohakkhayo—

translation_text.sn23.4:1.2: Then Venerable Rādha went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn23.4:2.1: “Rādha, I will teach you the things that should be completely understood, complete understanding, and the individual who has completely understood.
translation_text.sn23.4:2.2: Listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.sn23.4:2.5: “And what things should be completely understood?
translation_text.sn23.4:2.6: Form, feeling, perception, choices, and consciousness.
translation_text.sn23.4:2.7: These are called the things that should be completely understood.
translation_text.sn23.4:2.9: The ending of greed, hate, and delusion.
translation_text.sn23.4:2.11: And what is the individual who has completely understood?
translation_text.sn23.4:2.12: It should be said: a perfected one,
translation_text.sn23.4:2.13: the venerable of such and such name and clan.
translation_text.sn23.4:2.14: This is called the individual who has completely understood.”
